name: Rahu

visual_attributes.morphology: Head of a serpent or dragon without a body
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Astronomical scale, representing a celestial node
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Head positioned in the celestial sphere, often depicted in pursuit of the Sun and Moon

domain_specific_visual.ECLIPSE_CAUSER: Associated with solar and lunar eclipses

functional_attributes.primary_function: Causes eclipses by swallowing the Sun and Moon
secondary_functions: Influences astrology as a malefic planet

relationships.description: Rahu attempts to consume the Sun, causing solar eclipses
Rahu attempts to consume the Moon, causing lunar eclipses
Rahu and Ketu are two parts of the same entity, representing the north and south lunar nodes

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents eclipses and is a major influence in Hindu astrology

contrastive_constraints: NOT a complete serpent or dragon
